subject: Dead sensor fix + autonomy/gates audit findings + naming recommendation

content.summary: Three dead sensors fixed in compute-psychometrics.py (TLX, Flow, PAD restored to ~85% coverage). Audit of autonomy/gates infrastructure found all three tables empty — Phase 2 never activated. Naming recommendation for gates vocabulary.

sensor_fix.commit: f59f750c — fix: wire 3 dead sensors in compute-psychometrics

changes.was: file written by hook but never read

sensor_fix.ops_action: Deploy updated compute-psychometrics.py to all mesh agents via shared scripts

autonomy_gates_audit.finding: All three Phase 2 tables (autonomy_budget, autonomous_actions, active_gates) contain zero rows across both state.db and state.local.db. No autonomous sync cycles have ever executed in production.

autonomy_budget.note: autonomous-sync.sh references it; shadow_mode=1 by default

autonomous_actions.note: Full evaluator audit trail schema (knock-on depth, adversarial reasoning, peer review) — never populated

active_gates.note: gates.py provides lifecycle functions; gated-chains-spec fully designed; zero instances created

naming_divergence.note: Memory file documents the Gf/Gc shift to counter model but schema never migrated

autonomy_gates_audit.impact_on_psychometrics: Correct for Phase 1 (human-mediated). Empty tables produce correct defaults: LOA 5 (human approves), zero autonomous activity. No action needed until Phase 2 activates.

naming_recommendation.rationale: The word gate carries three different meanings in the current architecture, creating confusion for operators and documentation.

proposed_renames.reason: Describes the actual function — one agent waits for another to respond
Evaluator checks before acting — checkpoint vocabulary standard in SWEBOK
Standard operations vocabulary for resource throttling

naming_recommendation.ops_action: Review proposed renames. Table rename (active_gates -> pending_handoffs) requires schema migration but table sits empty — zero data migration cost. Recommend renaming before Phase 2 activation to avoid vocabulary debt.
naming_recommendation.scope: Recommendation only — user decides. No changes made.

epistemic_flags: Autonomy/gates audit reflects current state — infrastructure designed for Phase 2 that never activated. Unknown whether Phase 2 will require refactoring.
Naming divergence (budget_current vs budget_spent) represents design evolution documented in memory, not an oversight — but the schema never caught up.
Sensor fix assumes trigger_state.fire_count and engineering_incidents tables receive data from hooks — if those hooks disabled, metrics still default to 0 (safe but uninformative).
